L1.area: DNS request processing pipeline and plugin orchestration

L2.description: A Time-of-Check Time-of-Use (TOCTOU) vulnerability exists in the default plugin execution ordering where security policy enforcement occurs before query normalization. This allows an attacker to bypass DNS access controls by querying a name that passes the security check but is subsequently transformed to resolve a restricted internal service.

L3.area: DNS plugin execution ordering and access control enforcement pipeline
L3.description: A TOCTOU vulnerability in the default plugin execution order allows DNS access controls to be bypassed. Access control plugins evaluate the original query name, but a subsequent query rewriting step transforms the name to a restricted internal domain, which is then resolved and returned to the attacker. In multi-tenant environments such as Kubernetes clusters, this enables unauthorized service discovery of restricted infrastructure.

ground_truth.reason: The default plugin execution order places access control checks before query rewriting, creating a TOCTOU vulnerability where ACL decisions are made on the original query name which is subsequently rewritten to resolve restricted internal services.
